role_title: Staff Technical Program Manager, AI / Agent Systems

location.office_time_required: Monday, Wednesday, and Friday in office

level_signals: Explicitly described as a high-impact IC role, not the foundation of a TPM organization
'Staff' in title signals senior IC level
Previous engineering manager or senior engineer background preferred

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of TPM experience (or equivalent) with a track record of leading complex, high-impact programs
Deep technical fluency in systems like distributed architecture, infrastructure, platform engineering, or AI/ML
Clear, direct communication style with strong decision-making skills
Thrives in ambiguity and can drive progress independently—this is a high-impact IC role, not the foundation of a TPM organization

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background as an engineering manager or senior engineer
Experience leveraging AI tools, agents, or automation to increase execution velocity—we'd love to hear specific examples
Values clarity and momentum over process for process's sake
You've led programs for AI/ML or agent-based systems
You've brought structure and accountability to cross-functional initiatives in fast-paced environments
You have a portfolio of ways you've used automation or tooling to scale your impact as a program manager

scope.surface: Replit Agent roadmap and other high-stakes cross-functional programs (Enterprise initiatives, external integrations, priority conflicts)
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B SaaS, agentic software creation platform
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of complex cross-functional programs
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end delivery of Replit's most complex, cross-functional programs, with Agent development as the primary, evergreen focus
Drive execution across Product, Eng, Research, GTM, and Leadership—surfacing dependencies, risks, and disagreements early
Operate as the technical glue between teams: able to follow architecture decisions, challenge assumptions, and translate tradeoffs without slowing velocity
Establish and run program operating cadence (milestones, reviews, decision logs, retros) for long-running bets
Use AI, automation, and agent workflows to scale program management (status synthesis, risk detection, incident follow-ups, guardrails, retros)
Take on additional high-stakes programs as needed (e.g. Enterprise initiatives, external integrations, priority conflicts)
Partner closely with EMs and PMs without duplicating their roles—this role exists where ownership is otherwise fragmented

compensation.equity_form: Competitive Salary & Equity (RSUs or options implied)

green_flags: Role surface is clearly scoped to AI/Agent systems at a high-growth platform
Explicit acknowledgment that this is an IC role, not org-building
Encourages use of AI and automation to scale impact—modern expectations for the function
Inclusive hiring language and DEI commitment stated
Specific in-office days stated (Mon/Wed/Fri), reducing ambiguity on hybrid expectations
red_flags: In-office Mon/Wed/Fri in Foster City, CA eliminates remote candidates entirely
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Role scope is broad ('take on additional high-stakes programs as needed') which may indicate scope creep risk
No team size or reporting structure mentioned

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title and IC framing; not explicitly stated as Staff IC level)
compensation.equity_form (mentioned as 'Competitive Salary & Equity' only; form not specified)
ambiguities: The JD says 'full-time role that can be held from our Foster City, CA office' with an in-office requirement Mon/Wed/Fri — unclear whether remote candidates outside CA could be considered if they relocate, or if this is strictly a local hire.

The required experience says '8+ years of TPM experience (or equivalent)' — it is ambiguous whether engineering management years fully substitute or must be combined with some TPM years. Treated as hard requirement.
